plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD) exposure.
Significantly reduce broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Add a strong financial sector (XLF) for diversification.
Increase allocation to income-focused ETF (QQQI).
Hold a substantial cash buffer for safety.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdowns. The large cash buffer provides a safety net.

plan: Reduce leveraged ETF exposure (SOXL) and eliminate the riskiest (SPAL).
Trim semiconductor (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD) holdings.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Add a strong financial sector (XLF) for diversification.
Add a quality software name (MSFT) for growth potential.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% monthly growth by reducing leverage and concentration while adding diversification. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and financials. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for amplified returns.
Boost exposure in semiconductors (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD).
Reduce broad index (SPY) to allow for sector concentration.
Eliminate income ETF (QQQI) to focus on growth.
Add a high-conviction, strong risk/reward setup (PANW).
Maintain a small cash buffer, accepting higher risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by embracing leverage and sector concentration, especially in tech and semis. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the risk of amplified losses due to leverage and daily rebalancing of ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta names (CRWD).
Eliminate volatile semiconductor exposure (SMH).
Reduce broad index (SPY) to free up capital.
Maximize allocation to income-focused ETF (QQQI).
Add diversified financial sector (XLF) for income potential.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% monthly return through stable distributions, prioritizing capital preservation. It cuts leverage and growth stocks to focus on income-generating ETFs and diversified sectors. While aiming for low drawdowns, NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
